sourceTitle: How today's fastest growing B2B businesses found their first ten customers
text: Hacker News (which is how I found the product myself). After that, the biggest mechanism was word of mouth by \*far\*. > > At the time we hadn't even actually committed to primarily focusing on B2B — when I joined, we were doing both B2B AND B2C.” > > — Anonymous early employee ### Dropbox — Hacker News [Watch on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xy9nSnalvPc) > “Drew created a simple video, demoing the product, and **published it on April 2007 [on Hacker News](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=8863)** with the title "My YC app: Dropbox – Throw away your USB drive. That video brought the first users to the emerging Dropbox.” > > — John Popel ([source](https://www.reddit.com/r/startups/comments/9pgudv/how_dropbox_got_their_first_users/)) # Strategy 3: Press 🗞 ***Key question**: What’s a unique and untold story you can pitch press?* ### Canva [Watch on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3FZGN7BCs6k) > "Because we had some high profile investors we’d been fortunate to **receive a bit of press** around our ‘stealth startup’ and had 50k people signed up on our waiting list. We desperately wanted to ensure they liked our product when we launched.” > > — Melanie Perkins, CEO ([source](https://medium.com/canva/21-questions-from-aussie-startups-highs-lows-lessons-learned-during-canvas-journey-so-far-da07723ff545)) ### Twilio [![Image from How today's fastest growing B2B businesses found their first ten customers](https://bucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com/public/images/0089c533-4012-4b19-b93a-60b35f79d6ba_1914x1088.png)](https://web.archive.org/web/20090321112753/https://www.twilio.com//) > In November 2008, Twilio **[launched](https://techcrunch.com/2008/11/20/twilio-powerful-api-for-phone-services-that-can-recreate-grandcentral-in-15-lines-of-code/) on TechCrunch** in private beta. A couple days before the news, Dave McClure (angel investor and founder of [500 Startups](https://500.co/)) used the service to [rickroll](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oHg5SJYRHA0) Michael Arrington of TechCrunch, resulting in the company's [first press hit](https://techcrunch.com/2008/11/18/no-one-is-safe-from-the-rickroll-now/). ([source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twilio#History)) ### Slack > **“With help from an impressive press blitz** (based largely on the team's prior experience — i.e. use whatever you've got going for you), they welcomed people to request an invitation to try Slack. On the first day, 8,000 people did just that; and two weeks later, that number had grown to 15,000. > > The big lesson here: Don't underestimate the power of traditional media when you launch.” > > — Stewart Butterfield, CEO ([source](https://firstround.com/review/From-0-to-1B-Slacks-Founder-Shares-Their-Epic-Launch-Strategy/)) ## **In summary** **The three strategies to acquire your first ten B2B customers** 1. 👋 **Tap your personal network**: friends, investors, former colleagues 2. 🧗‍♀️ **Seek out your customers where they are**: online communities, offline events, door to door 3. 🗞 **Get press:** Orchestrate a launch **Key questions to ask yourself:** 1. Who in your network could be a potential customer? How could you quickly build a network? 2. Who are your target customers, and where they currently spending time, online or offline? 3. What’s a unique and untold story you can pitch press? That’s it for this week! [Hit me up](https://twitter.com/lennysan) if you have any stories, feedback, or insights to share. Otherwise, see you next week! [Don’t miss the follow-up to this post: [How today's fastest-growing B2B startups turned their early users into paying customers](https://www.lennyrachitsky.com/p/how-todays-fastest-growing-b2b-startups)] ### Thank you 🙏 [Andreas Birnik](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andreasbirnik/?originalSubdomain=sg), [Cailen D’Sa](https://twitter.com/cailen), [Cameron Walters](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ceedub/), [ChenLi Wang](https://twitter.com/chenliw), [Dennis Yang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dennisyang/), [Dylan Field](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dylanfield), [Frederic Kerrest](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fkerrest/), [James Beshara](https://twitter.com/jamesbeshara), [Joshua Merrill](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joshio), [Keenan Rice](https://www.linkedin.com/in/keenanrice/), [Kenny Mendes](https://twitter.com/kmendes), [Lou Kosak](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lkosak/), [Madelin Woods](https://www.linkedin.com/in/madelinwoods/), [Merci Victoria Grace](https://twitter.com/merci), [Mike Cannon-Brooks](https://twitter.com/mcannonbrookes), [Nate Bosshard](https://twitter.com/natebosshard?lang=en), [Nick Crocker](https://twitter.com/nickcrocker), [Peter Kazanjy](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kazanjy/), [Semil Shah](https://twitter.com/semil), [Shishir Mehrotra](https://twitter.com/shishirmehrotra), [Tai Rattigan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tairattigan/), [Tobi Lütke](https://twitter.com/tobi), [Tomer London](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tomerlondon/), [Vrushali Paunikar](https://twitter.com/artshali), [Zach Perret](https://www.linkedin.com/in/zperret/) **If you’re finding this newsletter valuable, consider [sharing it with friends](https://www.lennyrachitsky.com/), or subscribing if you aren’t already.** Sincerely, Lenny 👋
